topic: HL Extension: Philosophy of Religion (Arguments for/against God, Religious Language, Religious Experience)
subject: religious-studies

items.q: Why do Jews not accept Jesus as the Messiah?
items.correct: Because the Jewish Messiah is expected to bring peace, rebuild the Temple and restore Israel — events the Jewish tradition holds Jesus did not accomplish.
items.misconception: Because Jews believe Jesus never existed.
items.random_wrong: Because Jesus was crucified, and the Messiah cannot die.
items.why: Most Jewish thinkers accept Jesus existed as a historical figure. The disagreement is about whether he fulfilled messianic prophecies — a theological judgement, not denial of history.

items.q: What is the doctrine of the Trinity?
items.correct: God is one being existing in three persons (Father, Son, Holy Spirit) — distinct in person but united in essence; Christians remain monotheistic.
items.misconception: Christians believe in three different gods.
items.random_wrong: The Trinity refers to heaven, earth and hell.
items.why: Students treat 'three persons' as 'three gods'. The orthodox position is one God in three distinct persons sharing one substance — a notoriously difficult concept students often misrepresent as polytheism.

items.q: What does Kant's categorical imperative require?
items.correct: Acting only on maxims you could will to be universal laws, and treating people as ends in themselves, never merely as means.
items.misconception: Treat others how you would like to be treated.
items.random_wrong: Do whatever your conscience tells you to do.
items.why: The Golden Rule depends on your personal preferences (Kant explicitly rejected it). The categorical imperative tests universalisability — whether the maxim can coherently be a law for everyone.

items.q: Whose 1902 Gifford Lectures examined mystical experience?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: William James. “Rudolf Otto” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which 16th-century Spanish mystic wrote Interior Castle?
items.why: Correct answer: Teresa of Avila. “John of the Cross” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Otto publish Das Heilige?
items.why: Exact answer: 1917 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
